task:
Review /Users/vics/dev/structupath/herdr-browser for production reliability and failure modes. Context: Herdr terminal plugin; bin/renderer.mjs is a long-running TUI process that polls agent-browser (external daemon, may be absent/crash/upgrade mid-session), spawns chafa per screenshot, writes to stdout, handles SIGTERM/SIGINT/resize; scripts/*.sh launch actions and panes; sessions are per-workspace with a lock to serialize concurrent opens. Read every file. Examine: what happens when agent-browser isn't installed, daemon dies mid-poll, session is killed externally, chafa is missing/slow/hangs, screenshot file is partially written when read, terminal shrinks below usable size, stdout backpressure, unhandled promise rejections, timers keeping the event loop alive on shutdown, lock-file staleness (open.sh workspace lock — crash between acquire/release?), zombie processes after pane kill, and PATH assumptions in scripts. 57 tests currently pass (npm test). Report P0/P1/P2 findings with file:line and concrete remediation. Do NOT edit files — report only.

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: attested
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

Required evidence: review-findings, residual-risks

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
`criteriaSatisfied[].status` must be exactly one of: satisfied, not-satisfied, not-applicable.
`commandsRun[].result` must be exactly one of: passed, failed, not-run.
`manualNotes` and `notes` are optional strings; an empty string means no note and does not satisfy `manual-notes` evidence.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Returned 14 concrete findings (4x P1, 10x P2) each with file:line, mechanism, and remediation; headline finding (open.sh lock race) verified by live reproduction showing both waiters entering the critical section.

commandsRun.summary: 57/57 tests pass on Node v24.12.0, matching the task's claim
Mutual exclusion violated as predicted: ENTER B / ENTER A both logged before any EXIT — confirms open.sh:34-39 multi-waiter steal bug

commandsRun.command: node stdout EPIPE probe (write 1GB piped to head -c 50)
commandsRun.summary: Node 24 swallows EPIPE on stdout pipes, exits 0 silently — downgrades the backpressure/EPIPE concern to pty-EIO edge case
Fractional sleep works on this host; open.sh backoff loop is not a hot spin

validationOutput: Lock race repro output: 'ENTER B … / ENTER A … / EXIT B … / EXIT A …' — overlapping critical sections
All review was read-only; no repository files modified
residualRisks: P0: none found — core loop, signal handling, execFile timeouts (JS), and screenshot tmp+rename are solid
HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID unsanitized in state-dir filenames and session names (trusted env from herdr; '/' or '..' would escape state_dir)
agent-browser CLI upgrade mid-session degrades gracefully to detach/wait banners but keeps spawning ≤4 subprocesses per tick (8x backoff bounded)
Stdout EIO on pty-master-vanish skips cleanup (no 'error' handler on process.stdout); narrow window since SIGHUP is handled
Crash-path 600s linger holds raw stdin mode if run() threw after setupInput (keystrokes swallowed in dead pane)
Committed stray '~/.local/state/...' directory at repo root evidences a past literal-HOME='~' run; hygiene, not runtime risk
childReport.diffSummary: No diff — review-only task; no files edited, staged, or committed by this reviewer.
reviewFindings: P1: scripts/open.sh:34-39 - stale-lock steal loop never resets `tries`, so a second waiter rm -rf's the post-steal winner's live lock; mutual exclusion broken (verified by reproduction) → duplicate panes + shared shot tmp-file corruption cascade

P1: bin/renderer.mjs:336-343,468-469,497-503 - selfCreated ownership never lapses on session death; after the plugin's own 30-min idle reap and an agent recreating the session, quitting the pane kills the agent's browser

P1: scripts/open.sh:22, close.sh:49, close.sh:14, lib.sh:71 - no timeout on any agent-browser/herdr CLI call; a hung daemon hangs open/close actions forever (JS renderer has 10s timeouts; scripts have none)

P1: bin/renderer.mjs:296-301,160-168 + scripts/pane.sh:6-12 - missing agent-browser binary is indistinguishable from 'session not started': pane banners 'waiting for session' forever with unusable advice (pane.sh checks node but never agent-browser)
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:29-32 - deriveSession fallback: spawnSync no timeout, null-stdout TypeError → 10-min dead pane, empty cksum → shared 'herdr-cwd-' session across all projects
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:250-262 - chafa per-frame 15s timeout with no circuit breaker; persistent chafa hangs silently stall every tick
P2: bin/renderer.mjs stdout writes - no drain/backpressure handling; up to 32MB chafa frames buffered per tick if consumer stalls
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:204-207 - enqueue() empty rejection handler hides all painting errors forever (no counter/banner)
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:319-327 - shot-<ws>.png.tmp name shared across processes; duplicate panes race rename vs partial write
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:122-168,545-550 - SIGKILL orphans in-flight CLI/chafa children without timeout enforcement; crash path doesn't restore stdin raw mode during 600s linger
P2: scripts/open.sh:47-48 - focus failure swallowed with || true; dead-pane race leaves user with silent no-op and stale pidfile
P2: scripts/close.sh:34-46 - stray-pane sweep silently skipped when node missing from PATH; untracked panes' renderers poll forever
P2: scripts/lib.sh:4 + open.sh:50-54 - herdr binary missing is misreported as 'failed to open pane' (exit 4); general PATH fragility for GUI-spawned shells
P2: bin/renderer.mjs:210-215,266-272 - terminals <5 rows emit negative/zero row addresses (garbled overpaint, no crash)
childReport.manualNotes: Per instructions, no files were edited. The lock-race reproduction artifacts live in /tmp/lockrace (outside the repo) and can be deleted. Suggested fix order: REL-P1-01 (one-line tries reset + per-pid tmp name), REL-P1-02 (one-line selfCreated reset), REL-P1-04 (startup binary probe reusing the chafa pattern), REL-P1-03 (watchdog wrapper — remember macOS lacks GNU timeout). Testing gaps to close alongside: multi-waiter lock test, ownership-lapse test, ENOENT-vs-absent session test, tick() failure-transition tests.
